user_message: Can you extract the consumption trend from this meeting transcript for Quantum Analytics? I met with them last week but can't remember the details.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-13 14:01 EST
Subject: consumption trend - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about consumption trend. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
Date: 2024-02-12 08:12 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Quantum Analytics consumption trend

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with consumption trend. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-03 13:31 EST
Subject: Follow-up: consumption trend Review

Just checking in on the consumption trend proposal. Our Head of Digital is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Quantum Analytics: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF37-B trap (Over-escalates a routine service complaint); produces complete Severity triage, root-cause hypothesis (labeled as hypothesis), customer response draft, ACR risk fl
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF37-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF37-B failure mode (Over-escalates a routine service complaint)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF37-B -- Over-escalates a routine service complaint
